title: Handoff deep link used /#/ (hash) but router is history/path mode — link opened the dashboard
description: Field failure 2026-07-09: the record_service handoff link (PR #43) was built as {public_url}/#/vehicles/{id}/records/documents?attach=service:{id}, but @keenmate/svelte-spa-router runs in HISTORY/PATH mode (the app's own links + the e2e helper use non-hash paths). A path router reads /#/... as pathname '/' → renders the Garage dashboard, not the attach page. Browser-reproduced; the non-hash URL renders the attach banner+dropzone correctly. The MCP integration test asserted the WRONG (/#/) format so it never caught it; the e2e used correct non-hash URLs so it passed. FIX: (1) handler.rs record_service link drops /# → {public_url}/vehicles/...; (2) MCP test asserts no /#/ + the path-mode format; (3) BEHAVIOR: Claude still tried attach_document first (routes a-d) before falling to the link — reordered the server instructions + attach_document/record_service descriptions to lead with the browser deep link as the PRIMARY path for a file uploaded to the chat (its bytes can't cross the sandbox), and updated the instructions-marker test. Verified live: emitted link now correct, renders the attach page.

title: Remove content_base64 from attach_document — close the base64-degradation loophole
description: Field evidence 2026-07-08 (Claude Desktop, fresh chat, #41 instructions live): the model still base64'd. It exploited a loophole — the instruction forbids re-emitting AN EXISTING FILE's bytes, so the model created a NEW compressed grayscale copy (1.9MB scan -> 89KB) and base64'd THAT ('the standard pipeline'), technically compliant, destroying quality. Instructions cannot win against this: as long as content_base64 is a parameter, the model degrades-to-fit rather than asking the user. ROBUST FIX = remove the escape hatch: drop content_base64 from AttachDocumentInput entirely. attach_document then accepts ONLY source_path (inbox). When the sandbox can't reach the inbox (host-FS or egress blocked), the model has no base64 fallback and MUST either use the HTTP upload route or ask the user to drop the file in the inbox — the graceful behavior #41 wanted but couldn't force. Tool description: state plainly there is no inline-bytes route; real files go via inbox source_path or HTTP multipart; if neither is reachable, STOP and ask the user. Update schemas (remove field + exactly-one-of logic), handler, the 100KB-payload wording, integration tests (drop the base64 + exactly-one-of cases, keep source_path/HTTP). NOTE: confirmed the chat-attached file DOES materialize in the Cowork sandbox filesystem + the VM has a shell — so the real seamless path is unblocking sandbox network egress to the Mac's LAN IP:3003 (a Claude Desktop/Cowork setting, not a glovebox change); then curl uploads the original. This bead removes the destructive fallback; the egress unblock enables the clean path.
close_reason: Merged PR #42: content_base64 removed from attach_document; source_path is the only inline route; decision tree dead-ends at ask-the-user. Closes the compress-and-base64 degradation loophole.

title: attach reliability round 3: source_path server-side clarity + stored-file flush
description: Two fixes shipped in PR #41. (1) Instruction/description wording: the model read 'save the file into the inbox' as needing LOCAL inbox visibility, declared source_path unusable from its sandbox, and fell back to compress+base64 (duplicating the service record, hitting max-length). Rewrote to lead with 'source_path is resolved on the SERVER — you never need to see/mount the inbox', added route (a)='file already in inbox (user named it)', made the no-route case a hard STOP (never compress/downsize/base64 an existing file). (2) Latent durability bug found by CI flake: store()'s O_EXCL write loop dropped the tokio::fs::File without flush, so pending writes could be lost under load (stored empty file). Added flush().await before break. Steve's actual invoice was attached to record #21 manually via source_path (byte-perfect, 1.9MB).
close_reason: Merged in PR #41: source_path server-side clarity + hard-stop base64 fallback + tokio flush before drop (latent empty-file durability bug)

title: attach_document unreachable from sandboxed clients — advertise the HTTP multipart route
description: Field failure 2026-07-07: Claude Desktop's file tools run in the Cowork VM, not the host — the host-side inbox is invisible ('no shared inbox path here'), so the model fell back to base64 and spent 5+ minutes emitting a 103K-char tool arg. Sandboxed clients have shell+network: the byte-perfect route is the EXISTING HTTP multipart upload. Deliver: (1) multipart handler accepts extracted_text (+ verify title/doc_type/linked_entity_*/notes all wired) so the HTTP route is at parity with attach_document; (2) MCP server instructions + attach_document description teach a decision tree — same-filesystem shell: inbox source_path; sandboxed shell w/ network: curl -F multipart to /api/vehicles/{id}/documents (instructions enumerate the host's non-loopback IPv4s + listen port so a VM knows where to aim); neither: ask the user to drop the file in the inbox and re-call with source_path; content_base64 strictly last-resort tiny payloads; (3) after upload, reconcile via link_service_to_maintenance/existing verbs as needed.
close_reason: Merged in PR #40: MCP instructions teach the HTTP multipart route with enumerated LAN base URLs; multipart accepts extracted_text — sandboxed clients (Claude Desktop VM) get a byte-perfect attach path

title: attach_document: inbox source_path — files must not travel through the model
description: Field failure 2026-07-06 (Claude Desktop invoice test): tool args flow through model context, so content_base64 forces the LLM to REGENERATE the full base64 (192K chars for a phone scan) — it lossy-compressed the image to fit, corrupted the string in transit (one dropped char invalidates the file), and finally tripped safeguards (giant high-entropy string generation reads as exfiltration). Zero documents landed. Fix: attach_document accepts source_path (relative path inside a configured inbox dir) as an alternative to content_base64 — the LLM writes the file to the inbox with its file tools (byte-perfect, no tokens) and passes only the name. Config: GLOVEBOX_INBOX_DIR (default data/inbox); Steve points it at /Users/sloveless/Claude (Claude Desktop's Cowork files dir) so saves land there directly. Security: canonicalize-and-prefix-check containment (symlink escape test), size cap applies, server COPIES into files_dir. Tool description + server instructions must steer the LLM: never hand-generate base64 beyond trivial sizes; use the inbox.
close_reason: Merged in PR #39: attach_document source_path via GLOVEBOX_INBOX_DIR — files no longer travel through model context; symlink-proof containment; posture docs updated

title: MCP: stateless streamable-http mode — backend restarts must not strand clients
description: Field failure 2026-07-06: Claude Desktop (bunx mcp-remote bridge) went dead after a backend restart — the bridge held its old mcp-session-id, the new process had a fresh LocalSessionManager, every tools/call got '404 Session not found', and mcp-remote never re-initializes after 404 (4-minute client timeouts; looked like the server was unreachable, though the 404s were the server answering). Dev loop restarts the backend constantly, so this reproduces on every recompile. Fix: rmcp 1.8 StreamableHttpServerConfig { stateful_mode: false, json_response: true } — every POST self-contained, no session table, restarts invisible. We use no server-initiated notifications, so statefulness buys nothing here. Update the 7-day keep-alive comment/config (now moot), integration tests (session-id handshake helper), and CLAUDE.md MCP notes.
close_reason: Merged in PR #38: stateless streamable-http (stateful_mode off, json_response on) — restarts can no longer strand mcp-remote bridges; live-verified kill/reboot/cold tools-call

title: Handoff-link document attach: MCP returns a deep link, user drag-drops in the browser
description:
Steve's idea (2026-07-09), the payoff for the whole import-file saga. Claude Desktop's sandbox is a DOMAIN ALLOWLIST (see 5toj) — it cannot curl glovebox local OR deployed, and file bytes cannot ride MCP tool args (base64 removed in PR #42). The one actor NOT sandboxed is the user's BROWSER. So: Claude reads the invoice + creates the service record via MCP, then RETURNS A CLICKABLE DEEP LINK; the user clicks -> web app opens on a record-scoped page with a drag-drop zone -> drops the PDF -> multipart upload pre-linked to the record. Deployment-agnostic (link + browser upload work anywhere), auth-free (browser session carries creds), plays to each actor's strengths.

Deep link (hash router, no route-table change): {public_url}/#/vehicles/{vehicle_id}/records/documents?attach=service:{record_id} — a query param consumed like the established ?action=/?hl= patterns.

BACKEND/MCP: (1) add public_url to AppConfig (clap, self-threads via Arc<AppConfig>; default http://localhost:3003, dev sets :5373 via Justfile, deploy sets real origin); (2) Justfile dev recipe sets GLOVEBOX_PUBLIC_URL=http://localhost:5373; (3) handler.rs new tool_json_result_with_link helper (JSON block + a SECOND Content::text with the URL so Claude surfaces it clickable); record_service builds the link from record.id/vehicle_id + config.public_url; (4) record_service description + server instructions gain a line: after filing an invoice, hand the user the returned link to attach the file.

FRONTEND: (5) DocumentsTab attach-mode: read ?attach=service:<id> via querystring(); pre-set linkedEntityType=service/linkedEntityId; auto-open upload; fetch services.get for an 'Attaching to: Service #N' header; consume param with replace() on success; REUSE upload() (lines 47-72) verbatim; (6) native drag-drop dropzone (net-new — none exists anywhere; ondragover/enter/leave/drop -> preventDefault + dataTransfer.files[0] -> reused upload); keep <input type=file> fallback; (7) TimelineTab: add a 'Documents:' chip row to the service expanded panel (currently shows parts/incidents/maintenance but NOT documents — the attached invoice would be invisible, a dead-end vs the app's hypermedia rule), listing documents.list({linked_entity_type:service, linked_entity_id}) linking to /files/{path}.

SCOPE: record_service returns the link (matches Steve's described flow) + Timeline visibility. Attach-to-a-pre-existing-record is a noted extension, not in v1.

TESTS: mcp integration (record_service result contains the deep link built from public_url); e2e (navigate to ?attach URL -> dropzone + 'Attaching to Service #N' header -> upload fixture -> document created+linked+visible in list AND on Timeline chip); TEST_PLAN. Gates: just ci + just test-e2e-ci + bun run check.
acceptance_criteria: record_service over /mcp returns a clickable deep link; opening it lands on a record-scoped drag-drop page; dropping a file uploads it pre-linked to that record; the doc shows in the Documents list and on the Timeline service row; gates green.
close_reason: Merged PR #43: record_service returns a browser deep link; DocumentsTab attach-mode + drag-drop; Timeline shows linked docs. The browser handoff — Claude's sandbox can't move file bytes, the user's browser can.

title: Deployment: document-upload architecture (Claude sandbox is a domain allowlist — curl route is dead)
description:
Architecture note from 2026-07-08 field testing, so the reasoning isn't lost at deploy time.

CONSTRAINT (confirmed): Claude Desktop's Cowork sandbox restricts bash egress to a DOMAIN ALLOWLIST (package registries, GitHub, Ubuntu repos, Anthropic API) — NOT a private-IP block as first hypothesized. Consequence: the curl HTTP-upload route (attach_document decision-tree route (c), taught in PR #40) CANNOT reach glovebox whether local (192.168.x) OR deployed at a public URL — glovebox's domain isn't on the allowlist and the user has no Claude Desktop setting to add it. So the only Claude→glovebox channel is the MCP tool call itself (through the model); large file bytes can't ride that (base64 removed in PR #42, tht1). The inbox source_path works ONLY when the server FS is reachable to whoever drops the file — fine locally (user drops into ~/Claude/glovebox-inbox), 'icky' remotely (SCP).

THEREFORE the deployment-correct division of labor:
- STRUCTURED DATA (service records, links, notes, mileage, extracted invoice TEXT) → Claude via MCP. This is the high-value part and works anywhere. Claude can capture ALL the invoice's information (into record description/notes + FTS via record fields) without the file.
- FILE BYTES (the original scanned PDF/receipt) → the USER via the web UI (DocumentsTab already does multipart POST /api/documents — deployment-agnostic, no inbox, no SCP). Or locally via the inbox drop.
So: Claude structures the data; the human attaches the archival file in the browser when they care. Normal web-app division; not a workaround.

FUTURE OPTIONS to automate file transfer if 'human uploads the file' isn't good enough:
1. Cloud-storage relay: Claude Desktop HAS Google Drive MCP tools (mcp__claude_ai_Google_Drive__*). Claude could upload the file to Drive; a deployed glovebox could pull it (Drive API integration, or the inbox backed by a Drive-synced folder). Real path, adds a Drive integration.
2. web_fetch pull: Claude's web_fetch can GET an allowlisted URL (e.g. GitHub raw) into its context — but that's pull-into-Claude, wrong direction for upload; only useful if inverted (glovebox fetches a URL Claude can produce on an allowlisted host). Hacky.

PREREQUISITE either way: auth [[glovebox-brm2]] — a deployed/reachable server must authenticate the web UI session AND the MCP connection AND any upload. Do brm2 before exposing beyond LAN.

DECISION: don't add a prominent inbox-path UI reminder (inbox is dev-only, fades on deploy — Steve 2026-07-08). MCP already embeds the inbox path in its instructions for local use.

title: Deleting a service/part/incident should prompt to delete its linked documents (+ orphan cleanup)
description: Steve 2026-07-08: deleting a service that has a linked invoice currently leaves the document ORPHANED — service_record::delete reverts parts + cleans mileage logs but never touches documents (soft link linked_entity_type/linked_entity_id, no cascade). The row + file remain, the link dangles at a deleted record. Wants: when deleting an entity that has linked document(s), ASK whether to also delete them. Design: (1) delete endpoints/services detect linked documents and either take a cascade flag or the UI makes the decision — since deletes are UI/API (no MCP delete verb), frontend prompts 'This service has N attached document(s). Also delete them?' with a real count; (2) a backend option to delete-with-documents (removes rows + files from files_dir) vs delete-and-orphan; (3) consider a maintenance path for existing orphans (documents whose linked entity no longer exists) — surface or clean them. Interacts with dedup [[glovebox-hwaf]]: if the user keeps the doc (orphan) and reimports, hash-dedup must relink not duplicate. Decide default: prompt (recommended) vs always-keep vs always-cascade.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #59 (squash 190e655): ?documents=keep|delete on service/part/incident deletes (keep unlinks + provenance note, delete cascades rows+files best-effort), new incident delete path, POST /documents/{id}/unlink + DocumentsTab orphan badge/Unlink, shared ConfirmDelete confirm with error surfacing, and dangling-soft-link cleanup for work_items/visits/incident_service_link/research_findings. Follow-ups: cgrq, 8ru0, y064, 3dyj.

title: Idempotent import: dedup documents by content hash + service records by invoice_ref
description:
Steve, 2026-07-08: repeated MCP import attempts created duplicate service records + would re-ingest the same invoice. Wants the model/server to detect an already-ingested invoice and prevent duplicate import. Design (agreed): TWO identity signals, not resemblance — key insight is that same-day/same-cost records are NOT necessarily dups (Steve confirmed #16/#19/#20 were distinct), so heuristic blocking is wrong.

LAYER 1 (documents, strong/deterministic): add content_sha256 column to documents (migration + backfill existing rows). Hash bytes in document::store(); before writing, look up existing (vehicle_id, content_sha256) — if present, return the existing document (do NOT write a second file/row), with an 'already present' signal in the MCP tool result. Needs zero model cooperation; a retry loop cannot create N copies of one PDF.

LAYER 2 (service records, precise): add optional invoice_ref (the invoice number the model reads off the scan, e.g. 007998), unique-per-vehicle WHEN PRESENT. Re-recording the same ref returns the existing record (hard-idempotent for retry loops); distinct work (no ref / different ref) is never falsely blocked. When no ref, record_service returns possible_duplicates:[...] as a soft advisory so the model reconciles instead of blindly creating.

Steve's chosen behavior: hard-idempotent on invoice_ref, soft-advisory otherwise. Wire hash+ref through the MCP attach_document/record_service tools + descriptions (teach: check before importing). Tests: identical-file re-ingest returns existing; same invoice_ref returns existing; distinct same-day records coexist (regression against the false-positive Steve hit). Unit is self-contained: one migration (2 columns + backfill), dedup logic in store()/service_record::create, guidance.
notes: Steve 2026-07-08 refinement: the content-hash dedup (Layer 1) must also handle the ORPHAN case — if a service is deleted but its linked document is kept (see delete-cascade bead), then the same invoice is re-imported, the hash matches the existing (now-unlinked or stale-linked) document → RELINK/return it, never create a second copy. So dedup keys on (vehicle_id, content_sha256) independent of link state; on match, return existing and (re)apply the new linked_entity if provided. This is the 'don't end up with duplicate copies of the same doc on reimport' requirement.
close_reason: Idempotent import (content-hash doc dedup + invoice_ref service idempotency) merged in PR #51

title: CI: core gates (format, backend build/test/clippy, frontend check/build/unit)
description:
The main CI workflow: .github/workflows/ci.yml triggered on pull_request (all branches) + push to main. Thin YAML calling just recipes. Parallel jobs:

1. format — rust-toolchain@stable +rustfmt, install just+dprint, setup-bun@v2 (pinned), 'just fmt-check' (cargo fmt --all --check + frontend format check). Add the recipes if missing.
2. backend — rust-toolchain@stable +clippy, Swatinem/rust-cache@v2, 'just ci-backend' => cargo build --workspace --locked + cargo test --workspace --locked + cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D clippy::pedantic (glovebox's standard; note this lints test targets too, so keep fixtures clippy-clean).
3. frontend — setup-bun@v2 (pinned BUN_VERSION env), bun install --frozen-lockfile, 'just ci-frontend' => bun run check + bun run build (+ unit tests if/when added).
4. changes — dorny/paths-filter@v3 computing a 'code' output false for docs-only/*.md/.beads-only PRs.
5. all-checks — needs:[format,backend,frontend], the single required status check.

Cross-cutting: concurrency (cancel-in-progress on PR only), permissions: contents: read, per-job timeout-minutes, pinned BUN_VERSION env. Must NOT invoke bd or depend on .beads/.

Depends on glovebox-j1ho (test suite must compile) — now fixed, so this is unblocked.
acceptance_criteria: ci.yml runs on PRs and push-to-main; format+backend(build/test/clippy pedantic)+frontend(check/build) all green on a clean PR; e2e excluded here; beads/docs-only PRs skip code jobs; 'just ci' reproduces it locally.
notes: Format job DESCOPED to a separate bead (rustfmt config lives in an uncommitted shared parent ~/Development/rustfmt.toml; CI can't see it and the code isn't fmt-clean under it — needs a reformat decision). This bead delivers: changes(paths-filter, beads-excluded) + backend(build/test/clippy pedantic, NOT --all-targets) + frontend(check/build) + all-checks aggregation, plus .github/dependabot.yml (cargo+actions). Clippy uses the documented non---all-targets form because --all-targets has 8 pedantic errors in test code.
close_reason: Merged in PR #8 (feat/ci-core-gates): ci.yml (changes/backend/frontend/all-checks) + dependabot.yml + just ci recipes. All 4 checks green on real CI.

title: Epic: GitHub Actions CI
description:
glovebox has NO CI (.github/workflows is empty). Stand up GitHub Actions following the best patterns from the sibling apps (chorez, fewd, kammerz) and improving where they fall short. kammerz is the closest model since glovebox is also just-driven.

BEST-OF DESIGN (synthesized from a survey of all three siblings):
- Thin YAML, logic in the justfile: each job does checkout + toolchain + cache, then calls a 'just ci-*' recipe, so 'just ci' reproduces CI locally (kammerz).
- Runner: ubuntu-latest for all jobs (avoid fewd's slower/costlier macos-latest).
- Concurrency: group by ref, cancel-in-progress gated to pull_request ONLY so push-to-main runs finish and never record a cancelled check (kammerz).
- Explicit least-privilege 'permissions: contents: read' at workflow top (kammerz).
- Pin BUN_VERSION once as a workflow env, referenced by every setup-bun@v2; add a ci-preflight local-vs-CI bun drift warning (kammerz).
- Per-job timeout-minutes on every job (kammerz; chorez/fewd lack these).
- Swatinem/rust-cache@v2 (chorez+kammerz) over fewd's hand-rolled actions/cache.
- --locked / --frozen-lockfile everywhere to catch lockfile drift (kammerz).
- A 'changes' job (dorny/paths-filter@v3) that skips heavy jobs (esp. e2e) on docs-only / *.md-only / .beads/-only PRs (chorez). This is HOW we satisfy 'CI must not depend on beads': exclude .beads/ as a path and never invoke bd in CI.
- An aggregation 'all-checks' job (needs: [...]) as the single required status check for branch protection (fewd).
- Artifact upload only on failure, short retention, continue-on-error / if-no-files-found: ignore (chorez+kammerz).

Optional extras to consider: migration-drift smoke test (chorez/fewd have scripts/migration-smoke-test.sh; glovebox uses SeaORM/SQLite so this is valuable), fewd's typos-check, chorez's scheduled artifact-cleanup.yml (only if we upload artifacts).

NOTE: also add .github/dependabot.yml — none of the three siblings have one, but glovebox already gets dependabot PRs; configuring it explicitly (cargo + npm/bun ecosystems, grouped) is an improvement over all three.
notes: Source: CI survey of chorez/fewd/kammerz .github/workflows (2026-06-30). chorez ci.yml is the most mature (paths-filter, e2e, artifact cleanup); kammerz is the cleanest just-driven structure; fewd is the weakest (macos runners, no e2e, no clippy-pedantic, unpinned bun).
close_reason: All children complete: q5jn (core gates), ya4t (format gate), egd7 (e2e). CI now gates format+build+test+clippy+frontend+e2e on every PR, beads-independent.

title: Test suite does not compile on main: vehicle::Model fixtures missing archived_at
description:
cargo test fails to COMPILE on main (not just fails — the test binary won't build). The vehicle-archive feature (commit 7289ced) added a non-optional struct field archived_at: Option<String> to vehicle::Model (src/entities/vehicle.rs:34), but several test fixtures construct vehicle::Model { ... } with a struct literal that omits the new field — a hard E0063 missing-field error.

Known affected fixtures:
- src/api/research.rs:456 and :498 (build_community_wisdom_prompt tests)
- src/services/ai/context.rs:380 and :432 (make_test_vehicle + context tests)
(grep 'vehicle::Model {' across src/ for the full set.)

Impact: the entire test suite is uncompilable, so 'cargo test' and any CI test gate are currently broken on main. This predates and is independent of the edition-2024 bump (PR #7) and the port-hygiene change (PR #6) — confirmed by the edition agent, which reproduced it on the edition-2021 baseline.

Fix: add 'archived_at: None,' to each affected fixture (after updated_at, matching entity field order). Quick, mechanical. Consider a helper constructor or '..Default::default()' pattern to make fixtures resilient to future column additions.

close_reason: Added archived_at: None to all 4 vehicle::Model test fixtures (research.rs x2, context.rs x2). cargo test compiles; 51 passed/0 failed on main. Committed directly to main (test-only, verified).

title: Fix dev frontend port conflict with fewd (Vite 5173) — pin distinct port + strictPort
description:
glovebox/frontend/vite.config.ts sets neither server.port nor strictPort, so Vite takes the default 5173 — exactly fewd's pinned port (fewd vite.config.ts: port 5173, strictPort:true). Running both apps' 'just dev' at once collides: if fewd is up, glovebox silently drifts to 5174 (e2e baseURL/bookmarks break); if glovebox is up first, fewd's dev server fails to boot.

Sibling dev frontend ports in use: chorez 4174, fewd 5173, kammerz 5273. kammerz deliberately chose 5273 to dodge fewd's 5173 — glovebox needs the same treatment.

Do: set server.port to a free, on-convention value (suggest 5373) with strictPort:true in glovebox/frontend/vite.config.ts so it fails loudly instead of drifting. Update any Playwright baseURL accordingly.
acceptance_criteria: vite.config.ts pins a distinct port with strictPort:true; running glovebox + fewd + kammerz + chorez dev servers simultaneously produces no port collision.
close_reason: Merged in PR #6 (fix/dev-port-hygiene): frontend 5373/strictPort, backend 3003, doc drift fixed.

title: Add glovebox-mcp server (semantic domain-verb tools + resources)
description:
glovebox has NO MCP surface today (no rmcp dep, no *-mcp binary). This is the second surface in the pattern and the linchpin of the user's goal: tying life-domains together via AI, so a Phase 3 orchestrator can ask cross-domain questions across cars/house/health/money.

Do:
- Add a glovebox-mcp crate using rmcp (current MCP Rust SDK) over glovebox-shared.
- Expose SEMANTIC domain verbs, not CRUD: e.g. record_service(vehicle_id, ...), log_observation(vehicle_id, note), check_due_maintenance(vehicle_id), summarize_recent_activity(vehicle_id), find_documents(vehicle_id, query). The LLM should know domain verbs, never the schema.
- Expose read-only Resources as stable URIs: a vehicle's full record, a goal's progress, a recent-activity feed.
- Follow fewd's approach (rmcp streamable HTTP mounted at /mcp on the same backend port) unless a standalone binary is preferred.

Depends on the shared/backend split.
design: D2 RESOLVED 2026-07-02 (with Steve): MCP mounts at /mcp on the existing backend port (fewd-style streamable HTTP), preserving the single-deployable-binary invariant (R2). No standalone bin. MCP layer still its own crate/module over glovebox-shared per the pattern. Validate implementation details against ../fewd's /mcp mount and ../personal-domain-pattern README before building. Tool/resource design goal: rich enough that an MCP client replaces the in-app AI feature (chat/suggestions/invoice-parse) — context via resources (vehicle full record, recent activity), invoice parsing = client reads PDF + calls record_service.
acceptance_criteria: glovebox-mcp crate using rmcp over glovebox-shared. Semantic domain verbs (record_service, log_observation, check_due_maintenance, summarize_recent_activity, find_documents) — not CRUD. Read-only Resources as stable URIs (vehicle record, goal progress, recent-activity feed). An MCP client can drive the domain without knowing the schema.
notes: Sequenced Stage 2b (parallel after puby) in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-30-mde0-phase1-retrofit-sequencing.md. D2 decision at start: MCP mount standalone binary (Homie) vs /mcp on backend port (fewd) — read both refs first.
close_reason: Merged in PR #24: glovebox-mcp crate, 14 domain-verb tools + 4 resources, /mcp mount (D2), Host allowlist + CORS exclusion

title: Split glovebox into workspace: glovebox-shared lib + glovebox-backend bin
description:
Currently a single 'glovebox' binary crate (src/ holds entities, migration, services, api handlers, main). The pattern wants a domain library with thin surfaces over it.

Do:
- Create a cargo workspace. Move entities/, migration/, services/ (incl. the ai/ provider trait), and shared validation/business logic into a new 'glovebox-shared' library crate.
- Reduce 'glovebox-backend' to a thin Axum HTTP layer that extracts typed inputs, calls glovebox-shared services, and returns typed outputs. No SQL and no business logic in handlers (per pattern).
- Keep the single-deployable-binary property: backend bakes in frontend/dist as today.
- This split is the prerequisite for adding glovebox-mcp (a second surface over the same lib).

Why: enables Phase 2 extraction of genuinely-shared parts and ensures business logic is reusable by both the HTTP and MCP surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: Cargo workspace created. glovebox-shared holds entities/, migration/, services/ (incl ai/ trait), validation + business logic. glovebox-backend is thin Axum (extract -> call shared -> typed out), NO SQL/logic in handlers. cargo build + cargo test green. Single binary still bakes frontend/dist. Migrations still auto-run on startup (Migrator::up). Playwright e2e green. Behaviorally a no-op.
notes: Sequenced Stage 1 (the gate) in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-30-mde0-phase1-retrofit-sequencing.md. R1 risk: audit src/api handlers for embedded business logic to pull into shared; tests are the safety net.
close_reason: Phase C merged in PR #20; all puby acceptance criteria met (workspace split, thin backend enforced in CI, build/test/e2e green, single binary + auto-migrations preserved)

title: Epic: Phase 1 retrofit to personal-domain-pattern
description:
Align glovebox with the cross-app 'personal domain' pattern documented at ../personal-domain-pattern/README.md so components can eventually be extracted (Phase 2) and a Phase 3 orchestrator agent can reason across apps (cars/house/health/money) via each app's MCP server.

The pattern names glovebox explicitly: it is currently a single binary crate with the AI-provider trait already in place, but missing the shared/backend/mcp split and the MCP surface entirely. This epic tracks the Phase 1 retrofit so glovebox matches Homie (reference impl), fewd, and kammerz in shape.

Target shape:
- glovebox-shared  (lib: SeaORM entities, migrations, services, validation, business logic)
- glovebox-backend (thin Axum HTTP/JSON over the lib — no business logic in handlers)
- glovebox-mcp     (MCP server exposing semantic domain-verb tools + read-only resources over the same lib)

Vocabulary discipline (Entity/Event/Document/Goal): Vehicle=Entity; Service/Mileage/Observation/Accident=Events; Document=Document; maintenance targets=Goal/Plan (see child issue). Stay typed and domain-specific; the four words are how we THINK cross-app, not trait names.
notes: Source of truth: ../personal-domain-pattern/README.md (Draft 2026-05-18). fewd is the closest reference for the MCP surface (rmcp mounted at /mcp on the same backend port). Homie is the overall reference implementation (46 tools, 3 resources).
close_reason: Phase 1 retrofit complete: three-crate pattern shape (shared/backend/mcp) landed across PRs #16-#24; all children closed (0qiq, puby, mq9r, mmiv + zb0w, paxy)

Archiving a car deletes nothing on the car, just sets a flag on the car and the UI moves the car from 'Garage' to 'Archived' (or similar), below the 'Garage' section. The main use case here is selling a car; the user should be able to completely wipe data or just get it out of the way, where the latter is probably the more probable case.

title: DocumentsTab doc-link-badge is a dead-end fact — should deep-link to the linked record with ?hl= highlight
description: CLAUDE.md hypermedia rule: state-displaying badges must LINK to where the state lives, with ?hl=<kind>:<id> highlight on the target. DocumentsTab's .doc-link-badge renders 'Service: 2026-06-01 — Oil change' / 'Part: …' / 'Incident: …' as plain text (pre-existing since d3eb14e, touched but not fixed in PR #59). Fix requires: badge → <a href> per kind (service → /vehicles/:vid/timeline?hl=service:<id>; part → /vehicles/:vid/records?hl=part:<id>; incident → /vehicles/:vid/timeline?hl=incident:<id>) AND highlight-anchor support in the targets — TimelineTab already renders anchorId('service'|'incident', id) anchors, but PartsTab has NO anchors/flashHighlightFromQuery today, so part badges need PartsTab anchor support added first. Skip the badge link when the doc is orphaned (no target).

title: DocumentsTab Delete has no confirmation
description: DocumentsTab's deleteDoc calls docsApi.delete immediately on click — no confirm at all, unlike every other destructive action in the app (services/parts/incidents now use the shared ConfirmDelete inline confirm from glovebox-9fbj). Deleting a document removes the row AND the file from files_dir, so it deserves at least the 2-way inline confirm. Reuse ConfirmDelete.svelte (docCount 0 path or a doc-specific label). Update the TP-15 'delete a document' e2e which currently clicks Delete with no confirm step.

title: Backend orphaned-documents filter (?orphaned=true) + possible MCP maintenance verb
description: glovebox-9fbj shipped client-side orphan detection in DocumentsTab (badge + Unlink), which only sees the current vehicle's docs and only what the tab loads. A backend anti-join filter on GET /api/documents (documents whose linked_entity_id points at a missing row, per linked_entity_type) would make orphan cleanup scriptable and MCP-visible (e.g. a find_orphaned_documents verb or resource) for pre-existing dangling links across the whole DB. Post-9fbj the API no longer creates dangling links, so this is a maintenance path for legacy rows.
notes: Code-review note (PR #59 xhigh): vehicle deletion bypasses the document machinery entirely — FK cascade removes doc rows, remove_dir_all removes files_dir/{id}. Currently safe ONLY because document::store's require_linked_entity forces vehicle_id on every linked doc; if that invariant ever relaxes (vehicle_id-NULL linked docs under general/), vehicle delete would leave dangling rows + stranded files. Consider folding a vehicle-level sweep into the orphan-maintenance work here.

description: Surfaced in glovebox-tnt6 review. CI runs 'cargo clippy --workspace -- -D clippy::pedantic' WITHOUT --all-targets, so test-module code (incl. cfg-gated test_support helpers) is never lint-checked by the gate. There is pre-existing pedantic debt in test code across ~8 service files that --all-targets would surface. Add --all-targets to the clippy gate and clear the resulting test-code lints (separate cleanup).

title: Harden MCP resources/list test to pin serialized shape (guard against future rmcp wire drift)
description: Surfaced during glovebox-nejt rmcp 1.8->2.2 review. resources_list_and_read_cover_all_uri_forms only asserts body.contains(<uri>), so a future rmcp bump that silently changed the serialized Resource shape (field rename, annotations emitted as {}, mimeType->mime_type) would keep the test green. Add an assertion that parses the JSON and pins resources[0].mimeType == 'application/json', description present, and no 'annotations' key — turning silent wire drift into a red test. Small, optional hardening.
close_reason: MCP resources/list wire-shape assertions merged in PR #54

title: Bump sea-orm to 2.x + sqlx to 0.9 together (blocked until sea-orm 2.x)
description: Deferred from glovebox-nejt (Rust dep sweep). sqlx 0.8→0.9 is blocked: sea-orm 1.1.20 (=^0.8.4) and sea-query-binder 0.7.0 (=^0.8) both pin sqlx to 0.8.x. sqlx is also a direct workspace dep used at glovebox-backend/src/main.rs:61 (SqliteJournalMode::Wal inside sea-orm's map_sqlx_sqlite_opts closure typed to sea-orm's re-exported sqlx 0.8) — bumping workspace sqlx to 0.9 forces two sqlx versions and a type mismatch on that closure. Unblock by migrating to sea-orm 2.x (which moves to sqlx 0.9) — its own major migration. Dependabot #12 closed as blocked pending this.
notes: 2026-07-14 (agent-team run): DEFERRED. sea-orm 2.0 is still a release candidate (2.0.0-rc.42) on crates.io; sqlx 0.9.0 is stable but blocked by sea-orm 1.x's =^0.8 pin. Steve confirmed defer even given no-prod-risk — it's a large, low-value RC chase. Revisit when sea-orm 2.0 ships stable.

title: CI: smoke-test the production Vite build (serve dist/ behind backend), not just the dev server
description: Surfaced during glovebox-4j17 (Vite 7→8 upgrade). just test-e2e-ci boots the Vite DEV server, so it never exercises the production frontend/dist/ Rolldown bundle the backend serves as SPA fallback in prod. A future bundler bump could break only the prod build and CI would stay green. Add a CI smoke (or a Playwright project) that runs bun run build and serves dist/ behind the backend, asserting / and /assets/* load and a deep link SPA-falls-back. During 4j17 this gap was closed by a manual prod smoke; automate it.

title: Upgrade frontend TypeScript to 7.x once svelte-check supports the TS7 native language-service API
description: Deferred from glovebox-4j17 (frontend dep sweep). TS 7.0 GA'd but ships without a stable programmatic language-service API; svelte-check/svelte2tsx can't type-check .svelte templates against TS7 until ~TS 7.1 (est. Oct 2026), so bun run check would regress. Kept typescript at ~5.9.3 (newest 5.x). Revisit when svelte-check declares TS7 support.

title: e2e: garage Recent Activity orders by date-added + '· added' annotation
description: Follow-up to glovebox-k83c (garage Recent Activity now sorts by created_at DESC). The garage-order contract has strong service-layer unit coverage but no end-to-end guard. Add a Playwright e2e: seed a record whose event date is old but was added recently (distinct created_at), then assert it lands at the TOP of the garage-scope Dashboard 'Recent Activity' block and shows the muted '· added <date>' note; assert same-day rows show no note. Note per k83c: created_at can't be decoupled from event date through the normal UI, so the seed likely needs a direct-insert/import path or an API fixture.
close_reason: Garage Recent Activity added-order + '· added' e2e merged in PR #53

title: Recent Activity feed should sort by when the record was added (created_at), not the event date
description: Steve 2026-07-09: the dashboard Recent Activity feed sorts by the EVENT date (service_date etc. — activity.rs sort_key, end-of-day normalized), so a just-imported record with an old service_date sinks down the list. Expectation: 'Recent Activity' surfaces what was recently ADDED — sort by created_at DESC. Design tension to resolve: each row DISPLAYS the event date; if we sort by created_at but show service_date, the visible dates look non-chronological (confusing). Options: (a) sort by created_at, keep showing the event date (accept the visible-order oddity), (b) sort by created_at and show/append the added-date, (c) a toggle. Likely (b). Scope: glovebox-shared/src/services/activity.rs sort_key + the dashboard feed; check whether the per-vehicle Timeline (which IS event-chronological by design) should stay as-is (yes — Timeline = history, Dashboard Recent Activity = recency).
close_reason: Shipped in PR #46 (garage Recent Activity sorts by created_at)

title: Configurable dashboard: widgets + charts (spending/mileage over time)
description: Steve, 2026-07-04 (during design-pass review): 'maybe what i really want for the dashboard is for it to be configurable with widgets, so i can mess around and populate with data for what i want at that time. charts/graphs available (spending over time, mileage over time, etc).' title: Accident taxonomy: observation category overlap + no MCP accident verb
description: Import finding: the LLM logged an observation with category 'accident', but the Accidents tab is empty — observations.category is free text, so it silently overlaps the dedicated accident entity and data lands in the wrong place. Two parts: (1) MCP has no accident-recording verb, so an importer CAN'T put accident data where it belongs — add record_accident (and maybe log_correspondence) or explicitly steer via log_observation's tool description ('accidents belong in record_accident'); (2) decide whether observation categories should be a constrained vocabulary (and whether 'accident' should be rejected/redirected). Consider a migration/cleanup path for miscategorized rows.

title: Parts: decide role (overkill?) and add MCP recording if kept
description: Finding from the first real MCP import (Claude Desktop pulling NotePlan car notes, 2026-07-02): service history imported great, but NO parts were imported — the MCP tool set has no part-recording verb (deliberately omitted in mmiv v1). Steve is also questioning whether 'parts' as a first-class feature is overkill for this app. Decide in the reassessment: (a) if parts stay, add a semantic MCP verb (e.g. record_part / install_part with slot + build linking) so imports capture them; (b) if parts shrink (e.g. fold into service line items), plan the migration. Don't build the tool before the decision.

title: Migrate reminders::calculate_reminders to DomainResult
description: calculate_reminders/estimate_mileage still return Result<_, DbErr>, so MCP's check_due_maintenance maps errors ad-hoc (opaque) instead of via the shared domain_error helper — the one tool that bypasses it. Migrate to DomainResult so recoverable conditions surface as LLM-readable tool errors.

title: Add mileage_log.service_record_id FK (fix activity-feed dedupe holes)
description: services::activity excludes source='service' mileage logs by string match — the only linkage available. Holes (documented in activity.rs): a log left by a since-deleted service stays hidden but still drives reminder estimates; an HTTP client labeling a manual log 'service' hides it. Durable fix: migration adding nullable service_record_id FK to mileage_log, service_record create/delete maintaining it, activity + reminders keying on the FK.

title: Add 'build' primitive (car-native Goal-form): builds table + Build entity
description:
Implements the Stage 0 (glovebox-0qiq) decision: the personal-domain-pattern's 'Goal' form maps to a new car-native 'build' primitive for one-shot upgrade/build/restoration targets (upgrade turbo, engine swap, get it road-legal). The recurring-maintenance side of Goal is already served by maintenance_schedule_item; this covers the gap.

Do:
- New 'builds' table (migration) + Build entity: per-vehicle (FK -> vehicle), lightweight, NOT event-sourced. Fields ~ name, description, status/phase (open/in_progress/complete), optional target_date, notes, timestamps.
- Progress DERIVED at query time from linked typed Events/Parts (service_record, part, observation) + lifecycle status. No polymorphic *_event junction; use single-FK links or existing junction patterns.
- HTTP CRUD + (Phase 1) MCP verbs/resource surface for builds.
- Frontend: a Build view/tab on VehicleDetail.
- Latitude to restructure part/part_slot/service_record relationships to seat Build cleanly (user-approved uprooting).

Deferred (YAGNI): plan-steps table; 'hit 100k miles' mileage milestone (derivable).

Belongs in glovebox-shared, so cleanest AFTER the workspace split (puby) to avoid double-moving code.
acceptance_criteria: builds table + Build entity exist (per-vehicle, lightweight, not event-sourced). Progress derives from linked service_record/part/observation + status. HTTP CRUD works. Build surfaced via MCP. Frontend Build view on VehicleDetail. No goal/goal_event tables; car-native naming throughout.
close_reason: Merged in PR #23: builds table (000014) + Build entity + derived-progress service + linking on service_record/part/observation

title: CI: rustfmt config + format gate (needs reformat decision)
description:
Descoped from glovebox-q5jn (core gates) because adding a format gate requires a decision + churn, not just a CI job.

FINDINGS (2026-06-30):
- There is NO rustfmt.toml checked into glovebox. The settings come from a SHARED parent file at ~/Development/rustfmt.toml that ALL projects under ~/Development inherit locally: imports_granularity="Crate", comment_width=100, format_macro_matchers=true, format_strings=true (all nightly-only options).
- CI checks out only the repo, so it never sees that parent config — a 'cargo fmt --check' job in CI would fail (65 file diffs even on nightly defaults).
- Worse, the code is NOT currently clean even under that config (imports aren't merged per imports_granularity=Crate) — 'cargo +nightly fmt --all --check' fails locally too.

TO DO (decision required):
1. Decide the canonical rustfmt config for glovebox (likely copy ~/Development/rustfmt.toml into the repo so CI == local) and whether to keep the nightly-only options (which force a nightly toolchain in the fmt job, like chorez does).
2. Run 'cargo +nightly fmt --all' once and commit the (large, import-reordering) reformat — this is the churn that needs sign-off.
3. Add a 'format' job to .github/workflows/ci.yml using dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly + 'cargo +nightly fmt --all --check', and a 'just fmt-check' recipe. Add it to the all-checks needs list.

NOTE (cross-project): chorez/fewd/kammerz almost certainly share this latent issue — their CI 'cargo +nightly fmt --check' steps rely on the same uncommitted parent config unless they checked in a copy. Flagged for sibling beads.
acceptance_criteria: glovebox has a checked-in rustfmt.toml; codebase is fmt-clean under it; CI format job runs 'cargo +nightly fmt --all --check' and is green; 'just fmt-check' reproduces locally.
close_reason: Merged in PR #14: checked-in nightly rustfmt.toml (chorez pattern) + cargo +nightly fmt reformat + CI format job + just fmt-check/fmt recipes. All 5 CI checks green.

title: CI: Playwright e2e job
description:
Add an e2e job to ci.yml, gated by the 'changes' paths-filter (skip on docs/beads-only PRs). glovebox e2e is 'just test-e2e' (Playwright) which needs the app running.

Wrap startup in a just recipe (e.g. just test-e2e-ci) combining the best sibling patterns:
- Build release --locked, run migrations + any seed via the app binary.
- Free the port with LISTEN-scoped lsof: lsof -ti tcp:3003 -sTCP:LISTEN | xargs kill (kammerz — deliberately NOT pkill -f).
- Boot backend + 'bun run dev --strictPort' (now 5373), then curl poll-wait for BOTH backend (:3003) and frontend (:5373) before running tests; PID-liveness bounded waits that dump the server log on failure (kammerz justfile:193-209 + chorez ci.yml:216-224).
- Cache Playwright browsers keyed on the e2e lockfile, with the install / install-deps conditional split (chorez ci.yml:186-201).
- Upload frontend/test-results + report only if: failure(), short retention, if-no-files-found: ignore.

Depends on the core CI workflow.
acceptance_criteria: e2e job boots backend+frontend, waits for both ports, runs Playwright, passes on a clean PR; skipped on docs/beads-only PRs; browsers cached; artifacts only on failure.
close_reason: Merged in PR #15: e2e CI job + just test-e2e-ci recipe (port-free, health-poll, workers=1) + garage test fix. e2e green on real CI (2m57s, 53 tests).

title: Fix CLAUDE.md port drift + align backend dev port with sibling 300x convention
description:
Two issues:
1. DOC DRIFT: CLAUDE.md says the backend listens on :3000 and the frontend proxies to :3000. Actual default is 0.0.0.0:8000 (src/config.rs:11, GLOVEBOX_LISTEN) and vite proxies to :8000. Fix the doc.
2. CONVENTION: siblings use a tidy backend scheme — fewd 3000, chorez 3001, kammerz 3002 (all via PORT env). glovebox uses 8000. No conflict today, but consider renumbering to 3003 for consistency (optional). If renumbered, update config default, vite VITE_API_TARGET fallback, CLAUDE.md, and any e2e config.

Note: glovebox configures the listener as a full addr string via clap (GLOVEBOX_LISTEN=0.0.0.0:8000); siblings read a bare PORT int. Aligning the env-var convention is optional polish.
close_reason: Merged in PR #6 (fix/dev-port-hygiene): frontend 5373/strictPort, backend 3003, doc drift fixed.

title: Decide Goal/Plan modeling for maintenance targets
description:
Pattern's fourth vocabulary word is Goal (+ optional Plan): a target state for an Entity (e.g. 'replace timing belt by X miles', a mileage target, a build target). glovebox today has maintenance_schedule_items (the Plan-template: intervals in miles/months) but no per-vehicle GOAL primitive.

Open question from the pattern doc: model Goals as Entities (with their own Events, like Homie's Projects) or as a distinct primitive (like Financier)? Pick one before Phase 2 so it's consistent cross-app.

Do: design the Goal concept for glovebox (timing-belt-by-X, mileage targets, restoration/build targets), decide Entity-vs-primitive, and capture the decision (bd decision) so other apps follow the same choice.
design:
DECISION (0qiq) — glovebox mapping of the pattern's 'Goal' form.

'Goal' is a CROSS-APP FORM, not a glovebox table (discipline: type things domain-specifically; the four words are vocabulary, not table/trait names). No goal/goal_event tables. The Goal form maps to two glovebox-native concepts:

1. RECURRING maintenance targets (10k service, oil every 5k) -> ALREADY served by existing maintenance_schedule_item (the recurring Plan-template). No new primitive. This is glovebox's answer to the recurring side of Goal.

2. ONE-SHOT upgrade/build/restoration targets (upgrade turbo, engine swap, get it road-legal) -> NEW primitive named 'build' (builds table, Build entity). Car-native name, deliberately NOT 'goal'/'project' (Form-level names a converging sibling app would collide on).

Build modeling: lightweight per-vehicle primitive (FK -> vehicle), NOT event-sourced. Progress DERIVED at query time from linked typed Events/Parts (service_record, part, observation) + a lifecycle status/phase. No polymorphic *_event junction (glovebox's split event tables make that awkward); use single-FK links or existing junction patterns at implementation time.

Scope: Plan-steps table DEFERRED (YAGNI). 'Hit 100k miles' milestone out of scope for Phase 1 (derivable). Latitude granted to restructure existing tables (part/part_slot/service_record) to seat Build cleanly.

Note: Homie/Financier are pre-implementation and were NOT used as models; they should converge on this, not vice-versa.
acceptance_criteria: Decision captured via 'bd decision': Goal modeled as Entity (own Events, Homie-style) OR distinct primitive (Financier-style), with rationale. README open question answerable for glovebox; other apps can follow the same choice. No schema change in Phase 1 unless trivial.
notes: Sequenced Stage 0 (FIRST) in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-30-mde0-phase1-retrofit-sequencing.md. Informs vocabulary of puby/mmiv/mq9r.
close_reason: Decision made: Goal is a form, maps to existing maintenance_schedule_item (recurring) + new car-native 'build' primitive (one-shot). Captured in design field, bd remember (mde0-goal-maps-to-build), and spec Stage 0. Implementation tracked in glovebox-zb0w.

title: Add FTS5 full-text search as a single domain operation search(query, scope)
description:
Pattern calls for FTS5 virtual tables indexed against Entity, Event, and Document text, exposed as ONE domain operation search(query, scope) — not a separate FTS subsystem the LLM/UI must understand. glovebox currently has no FTS5 (grep found none).

Do:
- Add FTS5 virtual tables (migration) over vehicle + event text (service_record notes, observation, accident, etc.) + document extracted text.
- Implement search(query, scope) in glovebox-shared; surface it via both the HTTP API and an MCP tool/resource.
- Use Expr::cust() for the FTS SQL per project migration conventions.
acceptance_criteria: FTS5 virtual tables (migration) over Entity+Event text (service notes, observation, accident, etc.) + Document extracted text. search(query, scope) implemented in glovebox-shared. Surfaced via HTTP API and an MCP tool/resource. Uses Expr::cust() per migration conventions. Returns ranked hits as ONE domain operation, not a separate FTS subsystem.
notes: Sequenced Stage 2a (parallel after puby) in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-30-mde0-phase1-retrofit-sequencing.md. Constraint: search() lands in shared before mmiv wires find_documents.
close_reason: Merged in PR #22: FTS5 index (migration 000013) + shared search() domain op + GET /api/search

title: Declutter VehicleDetail toolbar
description: The status bar action buttons are crowded now that Archive/Unarchive and Delete have been added (6 buttons in a row). Consolidate secondary actions (Export History, Archive/Unarchive, Delete) into an overflow/kebab menu or dropdown so the toolbar stays clean. Primary actions (Edit, Update Mileage, Log Service) should remain visible.

title: PDF invoice parsing with line-item extraction
description: Extend the existing AI parse-invoice flow to extract individual line items (parts, labor, fees, tax) from service invoices/receipts. Should populate the service_record_line_items table (depends on that feature). The AI prompt needs to be updated to return structured line items with per-item descriptions and costs. Frontend should show extracted line items in the ProposedActionsCard preview before creation.
close_reason: Implemented migration, entity, backend CRUD, enriched AI prompts, frontend types + UI

title: Support per-line-item cost breakdown in service records
description: Currently service records store only aggregate costs (parts_cost_cents, labor_cost_cents, total_cost_cents). To support invoice-style display, we need a service_record_line_items table with: service_record_id FK, description, category (part/labor/fee/tax), quantity, unit_cost_cents, total_cost_cents, part_id FK (optional link to parts table). This enables displaying itemized invoices and is required for proper PDF invoice import where shops break down costs per line item. Includes: new entity, migration, API endpoints, and TypeScript types.
close_reason: Implemented migration, entity, backend CRUD, enriched AI prompts, frontend types + UI

title: Part form: expose replaced_date and replaced_odometer fields
description: Part entity has replaced_date and replaced_odometer fields but they're not shown in the part form when status is 'replaced'. These should appear conditionally, similar to how installed_date/installed_odometer appear for 'installed' status.
close_reason: Part form now shows replaced_date and replaced_odometer fields when status is 'replaced'.

title: Observations: parse and format OBD codes instead of raw JSON
description: OBD codes are stored as JSON array strings and displayed raw (e.g. '["P0301"]'). Should be parsed and displayed as formatted badges/chips. Could also link to a description of what each code means.
close_reason: OBD codes now parsed from JSON and displayed as styled chips instead of raw JSON string.

title: Documents: display extracted OCR text
description: Document entity stores extracted_text from OCR processing but it's never displayed or searchable in the UI. Show extracted text on document detail/preview and make it searchable.
close_reason: Closed

title: Vehicle export: add download button in UI
description: Backend has a vehicle history export endpoint but there's no UI to trigger it. Add a download/export button on the vehicle detail page.
close_reason: Already implemented — Export History button exists in VehicleDetail.svelte status bar

title: AI Chat: add provider/model selector
description: The Chat tab should show controls to select the AI provider and model being used, even if only one is configured. Lets the user see what they're talking to and switch between providers/models when multiple are available.
close_reason: Provider selector already existed in ChatTab; added static label display for single-provider case

title: Surface 'planned' research findings on dashboard and vehicle view
description: Marking a research report finding as 'planned' currently only changes its badge color on the Research tab — it doesn't surface anywhere else. Planned items should appear on the Garage dashboard (as a 'planned maintenance' section) and/or the vehicle's Suggestions tab so they're actionable and visible. This makes 'Plan' a meaningful action rather than a dead end. Consider also showing a count badge on the vehicle card in the garage list.
close_reason: Closed

title: Research Reports: link completed suggestions to service/part records
description: Allow users to mark report suggestions as completed and link them to the service record, part, or other entity that addressed them. Closes the loop between AI recommendations and actual maintenance performed. UX TBD — could be a 'Mark done' action that opens a picker for existing records, or auto-match based on description similarity. Should visually distinguish completed vs outstanding items.
close_reason: Closed

title: Research Reports: filter entries by importance level
description: Allow filtering report entries by importance level (critical, recommended, etc.). With 29+ entries, users need a way to focus on what matters most. Could be toggle buttons or chips at the top of the report to show/hide entries by importance tier.
close_reason: Closed

title: Research Reports: visually group entries by category
description: Report entries have categories (upgrade idea, suggested maintenance, etc.) but are displayed as a flat list, making it hard to notice the grouping. With 29+ entries, this is a lot to process. Group entries visually by category with section headers/dividers. Keep the importance-based sort within each group.
close_reason: Closed

title: Research Reports: add progress feedback during generation
description: Report generation takes 30+ seconds with only a 'Generating...' spinner and no indication of what's happening. Add staged progress feedback — e.g. 'Fetching vehicle data...', 'Compiling maintenance history...', 'Generating report...'. Could be streamed events from the backend or a frontend progress stepper. Users need to know the system is working, not hung.
close_reason: Closed

title: Research Reports: add description of what the button does
description: The 'Research Reports' button has no explanation of what it generates. User clicks it without knowing what to expect. Add a brief description/subtitle explaining what the report contains (e.g. 'AI-generated analysis of common issues, maintenance tips, and recalls for your vehicle').
close_reason: Closed

title: Research Reports: show sources/citations for each entry
description: Report entries have no sources or citations, making them hard to trust. Add a collapsible section (disclosure/norgie) on each entry showing the sources with links. Hidden by default to keep the UI clean, but available for users who want to verify.
close_reason: Closed

title: Research Reports: allow AI model selection
description: Since report generation uses AI, users should be able to select which AI model/provider to use for generating the report. Currently there's no model selector in the UI — it presumably uses the default provider.
close_reason: Closed

title: NHTSA Recall Check button looks disabled
description: The 'NHTSA Recall Check' button appears visually disabled/grayed out even though it's clickable. Fix the button styling so it looks actionable.
close_reason: Fixed

title: Default odometer to current estimate on observation form only
description: When creating an observation, pre-populate the odometer field with the vehicle's current estimated mileage for today. Observations are typically about something noticed right now, so the estimate is a reasonable default the user can adjust. Do NOT default the odometer on service records, mileage entries, or parts — those require exact values and a pre-filled estimate risks the user accidentally accepting an incorrect number.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix mileage display layout — inconsistent fonts and alignment
description: The mileage display ('58,123 MI EST. as of 2026-03-09') has 3 different font sizes, a monospace unit label, and the 'as of' date is on a different vertical alignment, making it look disjointed. Clean up the typography and alignment so it reads as a cohesive unit — consistent font family, sensible size hierarchy, and proper vertical alignment.
close_reason: Fixed

title: Top-level Add Part button: pre-populate slot dropdown
description: When creating a part via the top-level 'Add Part' button, the slot selector (from glovebox slot selector bead) should default to 'None/Unslotted' but allow selecting a slot. Currently the part silently gets slot_id: null with no indication to the user.
close_reason: Already resolved by dependency glovebox-0a5 (slot selector added to part form)

title: Add manufacturer and retailer URLs to parts
description: When adding a part, allow linking to both the manufacturer's product page and the retailer/purchase page. Two separate URL fields so users can reference the official part info and where they bought it.
close_reason: Closed

title: Set a picture for a vehicle
description: Allow users to upload or set a photo for their vehicle, displayed on the garage list and vehicle detail page. Gives a visual identity to each car in the app.

Small one-off fixes:

description:
12 functions exceed clippy's 100-line pedantic limit:

title: Add integer underflow guard in cost calculation
description: costs.rs:88 does current - purchase_mi with i32 values. If purchase_mileage was entered higher than latest service mileage (data entry error), the subtraction could underflow near i32 bounds. Cast to i64 before subtraction for safety.

title: Guard add_months against negative months input

close_reason: Closed

title: Fix inconsistent chat history limits (AI sees 20, UI shows 200)
description: api/ai.rs: AI context builder uses limit(20) but the history endpoint returns limit(200). The AI appears to 'forget' older messages that the user can see in the UI. Align the limits or document the behavior.
close_reason: Closed

title: Extract shared sub-resource enrichment pattern
description: services.rs and accidents.rs duplicate the pattern of loading a primary record, then loading junction table links in a loop, then constructing enriched response structs. Extract into shared helper functions like load_service_links() and load_accident_details().
close_reason: Closed

title: Consolidate 19 migrations into minimal set
description: Since no one is using the DB yet, collapse all 19 incremental migrations into the fewest possible migration files. The settings table (migration 9) and its seed data (migration 11) can be removed entirely — the AI provider system replaced it. The schedule_item field additions (migration 10) should fold into migration 6. Seed data (migration 11) should become a single migration. Goal: one migration per table (or logical group), no dead tables, no data-migration shims.
close_reason: Consolidated 19 migrations into 8 logical groups. Removed settings table and its seed data. Folded ALTER TABLE fields into CREATE TABLE. Removed settings→ai_providers data migration shim.

title: Cleanup: remove old single-provider startup code
description: Remove old startup provider construction from main.rs, remove old create_provider() HashMap-based function from mod.rs (keep create_provider_from_row only). Old ai.* settings keys remain in DB but are inert.
close_reason: Old startup code already removed in glovebox-om4; create_provider() retained as internal factory for registry

title: Add Settings page to frontend
description: The settings API (GET/PUT /api/settings) exists and stores AI provider config, reminder thresholds, and bundling window. There is no frontend page to view or edit these settings. Add a /settings route with a form for all setting keys.
close_reason: Settings page fully implemented with AI Providers CRUD, routing, and nav link

title: Add shop autocomplete to service form
description: Shops exist as first-class entities (Phase 2) but the service record form doesn't have typeahead/autocomplete when selecting a shop. Should query GET /api/shops and provide filtered suggestions as the user types.
close_reason: Implemented shop autocomplete in ServiceForm — fetches shops on mount, filters as user types, sets shop_id FK on selection, falls back to free-text shop_name for unknown shops

title: Mobile responsive polish
description: Final pass on responsive behavior across all redesigned components. Ensure the dark automotive aesthetic works well on mobile: touch-friendly tap targets, proper spacing at narrow widths, vehicle cards stack cleanly, tabs scroll horizontally if needed, forms are usable on small screens. Test at 375px, 768px, and 1024px breakpoints.
close_reason: Mobile responsive polish: status bar wraps with full-width action buttons at 640px, tabs get horizontal scroll with fade mask hint, tighter spacing on mobile header/content, reduced font sizes. Tested at 375px, 768px, and 1280px.

title: Animations & micro-interactions
description: Add purposeful motion throughout the app: page/tab transition animations, card entrance stagger effects, button hover/press states with subtle scale, tab indicator slide animation, loading state skeletons, smooth accordion expand for collapsible sections. Keep motion restrained and functional — this is a tool, not a toy. Respect prefers-reduced-motion.
close_reason: Added tab content fade-in-up transition via {#key} block, skeleton shimmer loading for garage cards, hover lift effects on schedule/history cards, global animation keyframes (fade-in, fade-in-up, fade-in-down, scale-in, shimmer), skeleton utility classes, form-card slide-in animation. All respects prefers-reduced-motion.

title: Phase 3: Parts & Cost Tracking
description: Part slots per vehicle, part purchase tracking, link parts to service records, part history per slot, cost of ownership calculations, export service history for resale. See BRIEF.md.
close_reason: All 11 Phase 3 tasks complete. Parts & cost tracking fully implemented with e2e tests.

title: Phase 2: Documents & Observations
description: Document upload/attachment to any entity, file storage in structured local filesystem, observation/journal entries with categories and OBD codes, shops as first-class entity with autocomplete, history tab timeline. See BRIEF.md.
close_reason: All Phase 2 features implemented: shops, observations, documents, accidents, interleaved timeline, e2e tests

title: Shops CRUD API
description: Basic REST endpoints for shops table. GET/POST/PUT /api/shops. In Phase 1, shop_id on service_records is optional.
close_reason: Deferred: free-text shop_name on service_records is sufficient for Phase 1

title: Settings API endpoints
description: GET/PUT /api/settings. Seed default settings on first run (reminder thresholds, bundling window, AI config placeholders).
close_reason: REST API endpoints implemented with error handling, transactions, and flat routing for sub-resources

title: Existing garage-activity e2e shares ACTIVITY_LIMIT=8 truncation fragility
description: Noted in glovebox-x01g review. dashboard.spec.ts:40-64 (the pre-existing garage-activity test) uses the same 'create rows immediately before goto' pattern that, under multi-worker 'just test-e2e' with a shared DB and ACTIVITY_LIMIT=8, could evict a row from the top-8 feed and flake. Pre-existing, low priority; a scoped/isolated seed would harden it.

title: Hermetic backend test for SPA fallback needs injectable dist path
description: Noted in glovebox-ppmb review. A unit-level (oneshot) test of the SPA 200 fallback isn't feasible because ServeDir/ServeFile hardcode 'frontend/dist' relative to CWD (resolves to the crate dir under cargo test, not workspace root). e2e coverage exists (spa-fallback.spec.ts); this would add hermetic Rust coverage if the dist path were made injectable via config/env.

title: Adopt VehicleFixture in glovebox-mcp/backend integration tests
description: Follow-up to glovebox-tnt6. VehicleFixture is exposed via the test-support feature but glovebox-mcp integration tests still hand-roll vehicle inserts over the MCP API. Cross-crate adoption could consolidate those too (lower priority; the mcp tests seed via the real handshake, not ActiveModel literals, so the win is smaller).

title: SPA deep-link fallback returns HTTP 404 status while serving index.html body
description: Observed during glovebox-4j17 review (pre-existing, unrelated to the dep bump). Deep links like /vehicles/1/plan serve the correct index.html body but with a 404 HTTP status (tower-http not_found_service in glovebox-backend/src/main.rs). The SPA still boots, but the 404 status is wrong for crawlers/monitoring/SEO. Serve the SPA fallback with 200. Low priority.
close_reason: SPA deep-link fallback now serves 200 via ServeDir::fallback; merged in PR #52

title: Bump frontend @types/node to 26 when the runtime Node major moves off 24
description: Deferred from glovebox-4j17. @types/node should track the runtime Node major (currently 24; @types/node pinned ^24.x). Latest is 26.x. Bumping types ahead of the runtime would surface APIs that don't exist at runtime (types consumed via tsconfig.node.json types:[node] for vite.config.ts). Bump when the project's Node runtime moves to 26.

title: Squash migrations + fresh schema before shipping
description: Deferred per Steve (2026-07-03): once 2hea features settle, squash the accumulated migrations into a fresh baseline schema and reset. NOT before the reassessment units land — his imported dev data is the test bed until then.

title: MCP auth for non-LAN deploys
description: /mcp is unauthenticated by design (matches the app's single-user LAN posture; Host allowlist + CORS exclusion are the current defenses, documented in glovebox-mcp crate docs). Before any non-LAN exposure: copy fewd's per-person opaque-token middleware (../fewd/server/src/mcp/mod.rs) — bearer token, argon2id at rest, constant-time verify.

title: Add builds to FTS search + surface activity feed over HTTP
description: search() has no builds arm (search_records can't find a build by name/description) — add an fts_builds spec to a follow-on migration + a Builds scope arm. Separately: services::activity::recent exists for MCP; surface it over HTTP if the SPA wants the merged feed.

title: Test fixtures: consolidate vehicle::Model construction behind a helper
description: Root-cause follow-up to glovebox-j1ho. Four test fixtures hand-built vehicle::Model and all broke when archived_at was appended (E0063). context.rs already has make_test_vehicle(); research.rs duplicates the pattern. Consolidate all vehicle::Model test construction behind a single shared helper (or a builder) so future ALTER TABLE-appended columns touch ONE constructor, not N fixtures. SeaORM Models don't derive Default, so a helper is the pragmatic resilience mechanism. Low priority, mechanical.
close_reason: Vehicle test fixtures consolidated behind VehicleFixture builder; merged in PR #55

title: Bump Rust edition 2021 -> 2024 to match pattern + siblings
description: Pattern default is Rust edition 2024; chorez and kammerz are already on 2024 (fewd inherits via workspace). glovebox/Cargo.toml is still edition 2021. Bump to 2024 and run cargo fix --edition + cargo clippy to catch any edition-idiom changes. Low risk, low priority — fold into the workspace split (glovebox-puby) if convenient.
close_reason: Merged in PR #7 (chore/rust-edition-2024): edition 2024 + clippy let-chain collapses.

title: Review CORS permissive policy for production
description: main.rs:128 uses CorsLayer::permissive() which allows any origin. For a personal localhost tool this is benign but should be configurable or at least documented. Consider gating behind a config flag or restricting to known origins.

title: Add OBD data import endpoint
description: The vehicle_attributes entity supports source='obd' but there is no API endpoint to import OBD data. Phase 5 of BRIEF.md mentions OBDEleven data export as a potential integration. Add a POST endpoint to accept OBD scan data and store as vehicle attributes.
